questions.question: What is the fundamental building block for sequential logic in digital circuits, capable of storing a single bit of information and changing its state based on a clock signal?
options: AND gate
Multiplexer
Flip-flop
Decoder
Inverter

questions.question: In a computer system employing virtual memory, what is the primary purpose of a 'Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)'?
options: To store frequently used instructions for faster access by the CPU.
To convert physical addresses into virtual addresses during memory access.
To provide a high-speed cache for page table entries, accelerating virtual-to-physical address translation.
To manage the allocation and deallocation of physical memory pages.
To buffer data transfers between the CPU and I/O devices.

questions.question: In modern out-of-order execution processors, what is the primary architectural benefit of employing 'Register Renaming'?
options: It reduces the total number of physical registers required in the processor.
It simplifies the instruction fetching and decoding stages of the pipeline.
It eliminates false data dependencies (WAR, WAW hazards) by providing multiple physical registers for the same architectural register, thereby enabling higher instruction-level parallelism.
It ensures that instructions commit to the architectural state in strict program order.
It allows multiple functional units to share a single common data bus without arbitration.

questions.question: What is the main objective of 'Design for Testability (DFT)' techniques in the context of ASIC (Application-Specific Integrated Circuit) design?
options: To reduce the chip's power consumption during normal operation.
To minimize the physical area occupied by the logic gates on the die.
To improve the manufacturing yield by reducing process variations.
To enhance the ability to detect and locate manufacturing defects and ensure the functional correctness of the fabricated chip.
To accelerate the simulation speed during the pre-silicon verification phase.

questions.question: In cache memory organization, what does the term 'set-associativity' refer to?
options: A cache where any block from main memory can be placed in any cache line.
A cache where each main memory block maps to exactly one specific cache line.

A cache structure where each main memory block can be placed into a limited number of specific cache lines within a defined 'set', combining characteristics of direct-mapped and fully-associative caches.
The policy that determines which cache line to replace when a cache miss occurs.
The ability of a cache to store both instructions and data in the same memory space.
